text: When working with data, understanding databases is essential. There are two main types: SQL and NoSQL databases. SQL databases store information in tables, which are made up of rows and columns. To retrieve or modify data, you use queries written in a language called SQL. Data modeling is the process of designing these tables and their relationships. Indexes are special structures that speed up queries by allowing the database to find data faster. In contrast, NoSQL databases are more flexible with their data structure, often using documents or key-value pairs instead of tables. Depending on your project's needs, you may choose SQL for structured, relational data or NoSQL for scalability and less rigid models.
